message:
fix(web): improve iOS Safari scroll behavior and use dynamic viewport units

iOS Safari has issues with nested scroll containers and 100vh not
accounting for the dynamic address bar. This causes scrolling to
feel "broken" on pages like the quote bank.

Fixes:
- Add global iOS Safari scroll fixes with @supports (-webkit-touch-callout)
  to enable momentum scrolling on all overflow containers
- Change 100vh to 100dvh (dynamic viewport height) throughout:
  - QuoteBankViewer scroll container
  - DistillationViewer TOC nav
  - RawResponseViewer TOC nav
  - globals.css .toc, .comparison-pane, .comparison-content
- Add -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch to scroll containers
- Change QuoteBankViewer contain: strict to contain: layout style
  (strict can break iOS scrolling)
- Add overscroll-contain to QuoteBankViewer to prevent scroll chaining

These changes improve the native scroll feel on iOS Safari by:
1. Using dynamic viewport units that respect the address bar
2. Enabling momentum scrolling with -webkit-overflow-scrolling
3. Preventing scroll chain conflicts with overscroll-behavior
